description:
secretx-core's WritableSecretStore trait contract (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1264-1278) requires: 'Atomicity: a reader must never observe a partially-written secret' and 'Failure isolation: if put returns Err, the previously stored value must remain intact'.

put with ?field= (lib.rs:268-275) does a read-modify-write at the Vault level: the implementation builds a new data map containing ONLY the named field and POSTs it. KV v2 has no field-level update, so this REPLACES the entire map. Two concurrent field puts on different fields race: T1 writes {a:1}, T2 writes {b:2}. Final state is non-deterministic and at least one field is dropped.

The README and module docs document the 'partial-update footgun' (other fields are not preserved), but they do not flag the concurrency hazard. Two well-intentioned callers updating different fields concurrently will silently lose data.

This is a maintainer-burden finding because: it WILL generate user reports ('I called put for field a then field b but field a is gone'), the answer requires understanding KV v2's no-field-level-update behavior, and the trait contract appears to promise atomicity.

Options:
1. Implement CAS-based read-modify-write loop using KV v2's metadata.version + cas write parameter, retry on conflict. This actually upholds atomicity.
2. Document loudly that ?field= put is NOT atomic with respect to other ?field= puts targeting the same secret_path, and that callers must serialize externally.
3. Forbid ?field= put entirely and require callers to read full → mutate → write full themselves.

File this with all three options noted; defer the choice.
title: Field put races violate WritableSecretStore atomicity contract
